rows.notes: All extractors agreed on this header row. The description and lack of monetary amounts are visually clear. No discrepancies were found.

rows.description: Gwinear Rec'd half a year at Lady Day 1795
rows.notes: Monetary amounts are consistent between extractors and clearly legible. The description from `gpt-5-mini` ('Gwinear Rec'd') is a more accurate transcription of the handwriting (specifically 'Gwinear' and 'Rec'd') than `gemini-flash` ('Gwineas Rect').
rows.english_description: Half-year rent received from the Gwinear estate at Lady Day 1795.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year rent received from the Gwinear estate at Lady Day 1795, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.description: Hildington Rect in Money Due L Day & Mich 1795
rows.notes: The description from `gemini-flash` ('Hildington Rect in Money Due L Day & Mich 1795') is visually confirmed as more accurate than `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Kidlington Rec'd...'. `gpt-5-mini` correctly identified that there is no monetary amount directly on this line, which is visually confirmed. `gemini-flash` incorrectly placed the amount 127.2.8 on this row; that amount is associated with the subsequent braced line.
rows.english_description: Rent or other money due from the Hildington rectory payable at Lady Day and Michaelmas 1795.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or other money due from the Hildington rectory payable at Lady Day and Michaelmas 1795; amount not stated.

rows.description: The same in Corne Rent Due Candlemas 1795
rows.notes: The monetary amount for the braced entry (covering this line and the preceding one) is clearly located on this line, as correctly identified by `gpt-5-mini`. `gemini-flash` incorrectly extracted no amount for this line. The description 'Corne Rent' from `gemini-flash` appears more accurate than `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Com Rent'. The pence digit is visually ambiguous (could be 6 or 8), but `gpt-5-mini`'s reasoning for choosing '6' (because it allows the sum of all entries to precisely reconcile with the final ledger total of 163.17.6) is compelling and strongly validated through calculation. Confidence is high due to the strong internal consistency.
rows.english_description: Corn rent from Hildington payable at Candlemas 1795.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Corn rent from Hildington payable at Candlemas 1795, amounting to £127 2s 6d.

rows.description: The same for Capon & Brawn Money Candlemas
rows.notes: Monetary amounts are consistent between extractors and clearly legible. The description combines elements from both extractors for accuracy: 'Capon & Brawn' (from `gpt-5-mini`) is visually more plausible than 'Lapton & Brawn', and 'Money Candlemas' (from `gemini-flash`) is visually confirmed over `gpt-5-mini`'s '(landmoney)' interpretation. Visual inspection also confirms this row has its own distinct amount and is not part of the brace (the brace only encompasses the two preceding lines).
rows.english_description: Payment for capons and brawn from Hildington due at Candlemas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for capons and brawn from Hildington due at Candlemas, amounting to £2 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts are consistent between extractors and clearly legible. `gemini-flash`'s description of 'Hildington Vicarage Rect 1795' is visually confirmed as more accurate; the initial letter is an 'H' not a 'K', and 'Rect' is a better reading of the abbreviation than 'recd'.
rows.english_description: Receipt from the Hildington vicarage/rectory for 1795.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from the Hildington vicarage/rectory for 1795, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts are consistent between extractors and clearly legible. `gpt-5-mini`'s description ('£ 163.17.6') is more complete and accurate, as the full amount is written out alongside the pound symbol for the total. The total value of 163.17.6 perfectly reconciles with the sum of all individual entries (30.0.0 + 127.2.6 + 2.15.0 + 4.0.0), strongly validating the pence value of '6' used for the braced entry in Row 4 and increasing confidence in the overall extraction.
